title: fraxinus nigra subsp. caroliniana
intro: Fraxinus nigra subsp. caroliniana, also known as the Carolina black ash, is a subspecies of tree in the Fraxinus genus that is native to the southeastern United States. The medicinal properties of Carolina black ash include its bark, which is rich in salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, and has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of using this plant are said to include reducing fever and pain, as well as treating various skin conditions and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Carolina black ash can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, although its use should be cautious due to potential interactions with other medications.
